# The only School of Interdisciplinary Forensics in the US

Pioneer and apply science to improve the legal system and protect people and society.

The **School of Interdisciplinary Forensics** at Arizona State University is **unified by our common purpose to pioneer and apply science to improve the legal system and protect people and society.** We bring together the broadest and deepest bench of expertise to produce groundbreaking and impactful research, innovate systems, and prepare students with the tools, knowledge, and collaborative initiative needed to address our most complex judicial cases and processes.

## Explore our programs

## Cutting-edge research

Our exceptional faculty at the School of Interdisciplinary Forensics is where expertise meets innovation.

Our diverse team of internationally renowned professors brings unique perspectives to their fields, conducting groundbreaking research in areas such as legal decision-making, genetics, and social influence in legal contexts.

Their work, funded by prestigious institutions and used nationwide, shapes the future of interdisciplinary forensics.

[Meet our faculty](/faculty/directory)

[View research labs](/research/centers-labs-initiatives)

![Two people using a microscope in a laboratory.](/sites/g/files/litvpz3591/files/2025-02/student-and-faculty-looking-in-microscope.jpg)

Inspirational, supportive community lays the foundation for future success.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Interdisciplinary collaboration enhances forensic medicine by integrating diverse expertise, leading to innovative solutions for complex cases and improving investigative outcomes.

Successful partnerships, such as those at Arizona State University, showcase how interdisciplinary teams can address unique forensic challenges through combined expertise in law, health sciences, and technology.

Future research will focus on developing advanced forensic technologies through collaborative efforts, fostering innovation that bridges gaps between disciplines and enhances forensic science applications.
